title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Gen Z, the online generation, is increasingly struggling with loneliness. Dating app Hinge announced a $1 million fund for social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London to put on free or affordable social events for young people as a global expansion of its One More Hour social impact initiative.
bullets: Gen Z is reported to be increasingly struggling with loneliness.
Hinge announced a $1 million fund for social groups.
Funds will support free or affordable social events for young people.
Initial target cities are New York, Los Angeles and London.
This is a global expansion of Hinge’s One More Hour initiative.
takeaways: The description includes no information about impacts on power grids or data center demand.
The announcement focuses on funding social events rather than technical infrastructure or digital traffic metrics.
No immediate operational or capacity decisions for grid/data-center managers are indicated by the provided description; further details would be required to assess relevance.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session demonstrates building a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents and connecting it to an AI agent so the agent can understand your business. The walkthrough is end-to-end and requires no prior graph expertise.
bullets: AI agents can perform many tasks but typically lack company-specific knowledge.
Knowledge graphs are presented as the solution to give agents business context.
Session builds a knowledge graph from raw documents automatically.
Connects that graph to an agent so it understands your organization.
End-to-end demo with no graph expertise required; prior episode link provided for additional context.
takeaways: Turns operational and technical documents into a format AI agents can use to answer organization-specific questions.
Automated, no-expertise pipeline can reduce the need for specialized graph-query or graph-theory skills when deploying agents.
An end-to-end demo and referenced prior episode offer a practical template to evaluate for grid/data center knowledge and documentation workflows.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description… In this video Randa Barsoum, Tax Partner at PwC US, highlights tax credits and incentives for sustainability beyond the familiar energy incentives. She emphasizes state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation (such as reimagining packaging or processes), and concludes that viewing sustainability investments through a broader lens can unlock meaningful tax value.
bullets: Energy incentives are common but not the only tax lever for sustainability.
State and local abatements can support sustainability projects.
Location-based incentives may be available depending on where projects are located.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation efforts like reimagining packaging or processes.
Viewing sustainability investments broadly can generate meaningful tax value.
takeaways: Don’t assume only energy credits apply—investigate state/local abatements and location-based incentives as part of project planning.
Consider whether sustainability-driven innovation (process or packaging changes) could qualify for R&D tax credits.
Evaluate sustainability investments as potential tax-value drivers, not just operational or environmental initiatives.

items.title: Copilot helps Christa find her inner star 🌟

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the video frames Copilot as helping Christa "find the star within" against a sky full of stars and invites viewers to download and try Copilot via the link in bio. It reads as a short, promotional/aspirational clip encouraging trial of Copilot.
bullets: No transcript is available for the video.
Theme: sky full of stars; Copilot helps Christa find her inner star.
Clear call-to-action to download and try Copilot via the link in bio.
Tone is promotional and inspirational, using a star emoji in the description.
takeaways: This is a user-facing promotional piece that explicitly invites downloads and trials of Copilot.
Decision-makers should note that marketing activities like this can prompt direct user trial requests and inquiries.
If trial volumes increase, infrastructure and operations teams may need to assess capacity and scaling readiness.
Coordinate with marketing/product teams to align campaign plans with operational forecasts and monitoring requirements.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This video announces the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build contest (u/JeffersonPutnam) and showcases their small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system built in a Fractal Design Era 2. The description lists the complete parts list, a PCPartPicker link, the GeForce host (Dwight), and social links for NVIDIA GeForce.
bullets: Contest winner: u/JeffersonPutnam’s SFF build featuring a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2; Motherboard: MSI MPG B850I Edge (mini-ITX).
CPU: Ryzen 7 9800X3D with 32GB Teamgroup T-Create Expert DDR5-6000 RAM.
Power and cooling: Corsair SF1000 power supply and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
Includes PCPartPicker parts list link and GeForce host Dwight (Twitter @_MightyPotato).
takeaways: High-end consumer GPUs like the RTX 5090 are being integrated into small-form-factor systems, requiring careful component selection for power and cooling.
The build uses a 1000W SFX power supply (Corsair SF1000), highlighting substantial power-delivery needs even in compact chassis.
A 240mm AIO (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) is used for cooling, indicating liquid cooling is a viable approach to manage thermals in tight SFF layouts.
Mini-ITX motherboards (MSI MPG B850I Edge) can support flagship GPUs when paired with appropriate power and thermal solutions, relevant for high-density or space-constrained deployments.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description, TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy GenAI solutions that unlocked new revenue streams while streamlining operations. The company leveraged Google Cloud's infrastructure to boost developer productivity and compress its digital transformation timeline, expanding what the business can pursue.
bullets: TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to quickly prototype and deploy AI solutions.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were used to streamline operations.
The partnership aimed to boost developer productivity.
Efforts unlocked entirely new revenue streams for TTEC.
Consulting helped compress the digital transformation timeline and expand ambitions.
takeaways: Invest in scalable cloud infrastructure to support GenAI initiatives and operational gains.
Engage cloud consulting to accelerate prototyping and compress deployment timelines.
Prioritize tools and workflows that improve developer productivity to shorten time-to-value.
Treat AI deployment as a potential revenue-driver, not just an efficiency play.
